description: An unordered collection of key-value pairs used for fast lookup, insertion, and deletion by key.
An ordered, mutable collection of elements that can be accessed by index.
An unordered collection of unique elements used for fast membership testing and set operations.
An ordered, immutable collection of elements, commonly used for fixed-size data or multiple return values.
A subclass of dict for counting hashable objects, returning elements and their counts.
A dictionary that provides a default value for nonexistent keys using a factory function.
A double-ended queue supporting fast appends and pops from both ends.
A key-value map implementation with constant-time performance for basic operations and no ordering.
A Red-Black tree-based implementation of the Map interface that stores entries in sorted order.
A set implementation backed by a HashMap that ensures unique elements without order.
A NavigableSet implementation based on a TreeMap, storing elements in sorted order.
A resizable array implementation of the List interface allowing random access and fast iteration.
A doubly-linked list implementation of the List and Deque interfaces supporting fast insertions/removals.
An associative container that uses hashing to store key-value pairs for average constant-time access.
An ordered associative container that stores unique key-value pairs in sorted order using a balanced tree.
A set container using hashing to store unique elements with average constant-time complexity.
An ordered associative container storing unique elements in a sorted manner using a balanced tree.
A dynamic array that supports random access and amortized constant-time append at the end.
A doubly-linked list providing fast insertions and deletions anywhere in the sequence.
A double-ended queue that allows fast insertion and removal at both ends.
A container encapsulating fixed-size arrays with access similar to native arrays but with STL interface.
A hash-based key-value map providing fast lookup, insertion, and removal.
An ordered map structure that maintains sorted keys using a B-tree.
A hash-based set used to store unique values with fast insertion and lookup.
A set that stores unique values in sorted order using a B-tree.
A growable array type offering fast random access and dynamic resizing.
A double-ended queue implemented with a growable ring buffer.
A doubly-linked list with efficient insertions and removals but slower random access.
A built-in hash table for storing key-value pairs with fast average-case access.
A dynamic, flexible view into an array supporting append and reslicing.
A fixed-size collection of elements with a known length determined at compile time.
A composite data type that groups fields of various types into a single unit.
A concurrency-safe communication channel for goroutines to send and receive values.
A concurrent map implementation that is safe for use by multiple goroutines without explicit locking.
A thread-safe FIFO queue designed for use between threads.
A module providing a heap queue algorithm (priority queue) using a list.
A queue that retrieves elements in priority order, typically implemented as a min-heap.
A thread-safe hash map that supports concurrent read and write operations efficiently.
A max-heap-based container adapter that provides constant-time access to the largest element.
A container adapter that follows the LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) principle.
A vector-like structure with a fixed backing array, avoiding heap allocation.
A buffer for reading and writing bytes, typically used in I/O operations.
A vector-like container that stores a small number of elements inline, falling back to heap allocation as needed.
